-> User: Calculate the density of a face‑centered cubic crystal with lattice constant a = 4.05 Å and atomic weight 63.55 g/mol, assuming one atom per lattice point.
-> Lattice constant a = 4.05 Å
-> Number of atoms per unit cell (FCC) = 4
-> Unit cell volume V = a³ = 6.64×10⁻²³ cm³
-> Mass per unit cell m = (4 × 63.55 g/mol) / Nₐ = 4.22×10⁻²² g
-> Density ρ = m / V = 6.35 g/cm³
-> \boxed{6.35\ \text{g/cm}^3}

user
Given a two-qubit Werner state ρ_W = p|Ψ^-⟩⟨Ψ^-| + (1-p) I/4, compute the concurrence for p = 0.4.
│
p = 0.4
│
Concurrence C = max{0, (3p - 1)/2}
│
C = max{0, (3*0.4 - 1)/2}
│
C = max{0, (1.2 - 1)/2}
│
C = max{0, 0.2/2}
│
C = max{0, 0.1}
│
C = 0.1
│
Final answer: \boxed{0.1}

A leaky integrate-and-fire neuron has a membrane capacitance of 200 pF and a leak conductance of 10 nS. Compute the membrane time constant τ in milliseconds.
байт
C = 200e-12 F
gL = 10e-9 S
tau = C / gL
байт

байт
tau_ms = tau * 1000
байт
tau_ms = 20.0 ms
\boxed{20.0\text{ ms}}

The decomposition of N₂O₅ follows first‑order kinetics with a rate constant k₁ = 2.5 × 10⁻³ s⁻¹ at 298 K. The activation energy is Eₐ = 103 kJ mol⁻¹. Calculate the rate constant k₂ at 350 K using the Arrhenius equation.

</think>
-> k1 = 2.5e-3 s^-1
-> T1 = 298 K
-> T2 = 350 K
-> Ea = 103000 J/mol
-> R = 8.314 J/(mol·K)
-> exponent = Ea/R * (1/T1 - 1/T2)
-> exponent = 6.176
-> factor = exp(exponent)
-> factor = 4.81e2
-> k2 = k1 * factor
-> k2 = 1.20 s^-1
-> \boxed{1.
